description_long: This is a medium-sized barn with a simple, rectangular footprint and a steeply pitched gable roof. The exterior is clad in horizontal wooden siding, giving it a classic rural appearance. One end of the barn features a single, centered door, while the opposite end is solid with no visible openings. The roof is continuous and unbroken, constructed from what appears to be wooden shingles or planks. There are no visible windows or additional architectural embellishments. The overall design is utilitarian, reflecting a traditional American barn style suited for agricultural storage or shelter.
description: A rectangular barn with a steep gable roof and horizontal wooden siding, featuring a single door and no visible windows. The design is simple and traditional, typical of rural American barns.
description_view_0: The barn is viewed from the solid end with no visible doors or windows, highlighting the horizontal siding and the steep gable roof.
description_view_1: This side view shows the long, uninterrupted expanse of the barn's wall and the full length of the gable roof.
description_view_2: The barn is seen from the end with a single, centered door, emphasizing the entry point and the symmetry of the structure.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, showing the opposite long wall and the continuous gable roof.
attribute_door_style: single hinged door, centered on one gable end
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
